howItWorks.label: How It Works
howItWorks.title: The Cooling Process
howItWorks.subtitle: Understand the working principle of industrial cooling towers in 4 steps.

step1.title: Hot Water Inlet
step1.description: Hot water returning from the system is fed into the tower from the top or side inlet.

step2.title: Air Contact
step2.description: Water contacts air in counterflow or crossflow principle as it passes through the fill media.

step3.title: Evaporative Cooling
step3.description: A small portion of the water evaporates, carrying large amounts of heat energy to the atmosphere.

step4.title: Cooled Water
step4.description: The cooled water collects in the basin and is fed back to the system, repeating the cycle.

towerTypes.label: Tower Types
towerTypes.title: Cooling Tower Types
towerTypes.subtitle: Open-circuit and closed-circuit cooling tower solutions aligned with Ensotek's product structure and core operating principles.

openCircuit.title: Open Circuit Cooling Tower
openCircuit.description: A classic evaporative cooling solution that rejects heat by evaporating a portion of the water. At Ensotek, these systems are offered in counterflow and crossflow configurations.

closedCircuit.title: Closed Circuit Cooling Tower
closedCircuit.description: The process fluid circulates in a closed-loop coil and does not directly contact the outside environment. Ideal where fluid cleanliness must be preserved.

counterflow.title: Counterflow
counterflow.description: In open-circuit towers, water flows downward while air moves upward. It offers a compact footprint and high thermal efficiency.

crossflow.title: Crossflow
crossflow.description: In open-circuit towers, water flows vertically while air moves horizontally. It provides easier maintenance access and a broad air intake area.

closedCircuit.description: Isolates process water from outside, prevents contamination, extends equipment life.

faq1.question: How is Legionella risk managed?
faq1.answer: Legionella risk is minimized in cooling towers through biocide dosing, regular maintenance and water analysis. Ensotek provides maintenance programs compliant with TSE and REHVA standards.

faq2.question: Which tower type is right for my project?
faq2.answer: We offer free technical consultancy based on capacity, available space, water quality and budget requirements. Our expert team determines the most suitable solution.

faq3.question: What are the advantages of FRP body?
faq3.answer: Fiberglass reinforced polyester (FRP) is corrosion-resistant, lightweight and long-lasting. It offers 40% longer service life compared to metal bodies.

faq4.question: How is capacity calculated?
faq4.answer: Cooling capacity is determined by thermodynamic calculation based on process load, inlet/outlet temperatures and approach values. Contact our team for a free calculation.

faq5.question: How often should maintenance be performed?
faq5.answer: Seasonal maintenance and 1-2 full maintenance per year is recommended. Continuous monitoring systems can be installed for critical facilities.
